text: 2-Phenylmalondialdehyde (PhMDA), chemical formula C9H8O2, molecular weight 148.16, is a single-component hydrogen-bonded organic molecular crystal and a room-temperature ferroelectric. It crystallizes in the non-centrosymmetric, polar orthorhombic space group Pna2_1 (No. 33), with unit cell a = 7.523 Å, b = 17.165 Å, c = 5.552 Å, Z = 4. Molecules carrying the beta-diketone enol O=C-C=C-OH group are connected by O-H...O hydrogen bonds along the [102] and [1 0 -2] directions; asymmetric off-center proton configurations break inversion symmetry and give a spontaneous polarization of about 9 microC/cm2 along the c axis, with a Curie temperature above 363 K (values from earlier ferroelectric studies). In this work, room-temperature terahertz radiation from PhMDA single crystals was generated by femtosecond-laser optical rectification, a second-order nonlinear optical effect; the emitted single-cycle terahertz pulse had an amplitude comparable to that of a ZnTe reference emitter, attributed to a long coherence length of about 130-800 microns. No piezoelectric coefficient was measured. The crystal structure (CCDC 1238105, CSD refcode PROLON) was determined by single-crystal X-ray diffraction at 111 K with an R factor of 3.2% and a density of 1.373 g/cm3. The terahertz-radiation study was reported by Guan et al. in the Japanese Journal of Applied Physics (2014).
property_ref_title: Terahertz radiation by optical rectification in a hydrogen-bonded organic molecular ferroelectric crystal, 2-phenylmalondialdehyde
